fields.name: General Works
fields.description: Encyclopedias, newspapers, and general reference works (000s)

fields.description: Philosophy, psychology, and ethics (100s)

fields.name: Religion
fields.description: Religion, mythology, and theology (200s)

fields.name: Social Sciences
fields.description: Economics, law, education, and politics (300s)

fields.name: Language
fields.description: Linguistics, dictionaries, and writing (400s)

fields.name: Pure Science
fields.description: Mathematics, physics, biology, and chemistry (500s)

fields.name: Technology
fields.description: Engineering, medicine, computing, and applied sciences (600s)

fields.name: Arts & Recreation
fields.description: Fine arts, music, sports, and photography (700s)

fields.name: Literature
fields.description: Fiction, poetry, drama, and essays (800s)

fields.name: History & Geography
fields.description: World history, biography, travel, and geography (900s)

fields.name: The Midnight Library
fields.description: A novel about infinite possibilities and second chances, by Matt Haig.

fields.name: Atomic Habits
fields.description: An easy and proven way to build good habits and break bad ones, by James Clear.

fields.name: Dune
fields.description: Frank Herbert's epic science fiction saga set on the desert planet Arrakis.
George Orwell's haunting vision of a totalitarian future society.

fields.name: The Alchemist
fields.description: Paulo Coelho's beloved novel about following your dreams and listening to your heart.

fields.name: Sapiens
fields.description: A brief history of humankind by Yuval Noah Harari.

fields.name: Thinking, Fast and Slow
fields.description: Daniel Kahneman explores the two systems that drive the way we think and make choices.

fields.name: The Great Gatsby
fields.description: F. Scott Fitzgerald's classic tale of wealth, love, and the American Dream in the 1920s.

fields.name: Educated
fields.description: Tara Westover's memoir about growing up in a survivalist family and her journey to Cambridge.

fields.name: The Power of Now
fields.description: Eckhart Tolle's guide to spiritual enlightenment through living in the present moment.

fields.name: A Brief History of Time
fields.description: Stephen Hawking's landmark work on cosmology, black holes, and the nature of the universe.

fields.name: The Lean Startup
fields.description: Eric Ries on how today's entrepreneurs use continuous innovation to create successful businesses.

fields.name: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone
fields.description: J.K. Rowling's magical story of a young boy who discovers he is a wizard.

fields.name: To Kill a Mockingbird
fields.description: Harper Lee's Pulitzer Prize-winning story of racial injustice and moral growth in the American South.

fields.name: The Art of War
fields.description: Sun Tzu's ancient Chinese military treatise on strategy, tactics, and leadership.

fields.name: Homo Deus
fields.description: Yuval Noah Harari explores the future of humankind and where our species is headed.

fields.name: The Selfish Gene
fields.description: Richard Dawkins' groundbreaking work introducing the gene-centred view of evolution.

fields.name: Clean Code
fields.description: Robert C. Martin's handbook of agile software craftsmanship for writing readable, maintainable code.

fields.name: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
fields.description: Douglas Adams' comedic sci-fi adventure following Arthur Dent across the universe.

fields.name: Guns, Germs, and Steel
fields.description: Jared Diamond's Pulitzer Prize-winning exploration of why some civilizations came to dominate others.

fields.name: The Bible
fields.description: The sacred text of Christianity, containing the Old and New Testaments.

fields.name: The Oxford Dictionary of English
fields.description: The definitive record of the English language with over 350,000 words and phrases.

fields.name: Introduction to Algorithms
fields.description: Cormen et al.'s comprehensive textbook on algorithms used in computer science courses worldwide.

fields.name: The Story of Art
fields.description: E.H. Gombrich's accessible and celebrated introduction to the history of art across cultures.

fields.name: Freakonomics
fields.description: Steven Levitt and Stephen Dubner explore the hidden side of everything using economic thinking.

fields.name: The Communist Manifesto
fields.description: Karl Marx and Friedrich Engels' foundational political text outlining the theory of communism.

fields.name: The Elements of Style
fields.description: Strunk and White's timeless guide to writing clear and effective English prose.

fields.name: The Encyclopaedia Britannica Vol. 1
fields.description: The first volume of the world's most trusted general reference encyclopedia.

fields.name: The Joy of Music
fields.description: Leonard Bernstein's witty and passionate exploration of classical music for the general reader.

fields.name: Crime and Punishment
fields.description: Fyodor Dostoevsky's psychological masterpiece about guilt, morality, and redemption in 19th-century Russia.
